description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun with a boxy, utilitarian design, featuring a prominent rectangular receiver and a straight, vertical magazine extending below the grip. The exterior is finished in a striking camouflage pattern of purple and white, giving it a modern, urban aesthetic. The stock is a skeletonized, wire-frame type, extending from the rear and designed for lightweight support, and it appears to be fixed in place. The barrel is short and stubby, with a small cylindrical muzzle device at the front. There is a simple iron sight system mounted on top, and a trigger guard is visible beneath the receiver. The overall construction suggests a metal body with some plastic or polymer components, typical of compact automatic weapons used in close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, boxy submachine gun with a purple and white camouflage finish, skeletonized stock, and vertical magazine. Features a short barrel, iron sights, and a utilitarian, urban-ready design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, skeletonized stock, vertical magazine, and camouflage pattern.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the muzzle, iron sight, and the narrow profile of the receiver and stock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the stock and magazine clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the stock and the back of the receiver, emphasizing the rifle's slim width.
